meta.title: Spacing between the enhancer and promoter of the long terminal repeat of a murine leukaemia retrovirus is required for transcriptional activation in T cells

abstractHtml: In T cells, transcriptional activation by the long terminal repeat (LTR) of the mink cell focus-forming murine leukaemia virus requires some spacing between the enhancer and promoter. A large size-range of intervening sequences (11-93 bp) is able to activate transcription efficiently. Neither a specific nucleotide sequence nor stereospecific alignment of the spacer is important.
